properties.remark: HAIL BROKE THE OUTSIDE PANE OF A DOUBLE PANE WINDOW
FIRE CHIEF REPORTED GOLFBALL TO TENNIS BALL SIZED HAIL COVERING GROUND TO A DEPTH OF 8 TO 10 INCHES UPON HIS ARRIVAL HOME.
RURAL FIREMAN REPORTED WINDOWS BROKEN OUT OF HIS HOME BY THE HAIL AND THE GROUND WAS COMPLETELY COVERED.
SOFT BALL SIZE HAIL WAS OBSERVED AT ROSE CHURCH. WINDOWS WERE KNOCKED OUT OF ONE VEHICLE.
THREE PIVOTS WERE OVERTURNED FROM THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS SOUTH AND ALSO SOUTHEAST OF BAARTLETT.
WIND GUSTS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 50 MPH.
NEBRASKA RAIN NETWORK OBSERVER REPORTED HAIL OF NICKEL TO GOLFBALL SIZE LASTED FOR 10 MINUTES.
NEBRASKA RAIN NETWORK OBSERVER REPORTED AN ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND, HEAVY RAIN, AND ROOF DAMAGE.
NEBRASKA RAIN OBSERVER REPORTED HAIL LASTING FOR 5 MINUTES, COVERING GROUND, AND MINOR CROP DAMAGE.
AN ELEVEN TOWER CENTER PIVOT WAS OVERTURNED DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
AN AWNING WAS BLOWN OFF A HOUSE IN BIG SPRINGS.

COUNTY ROADS FLOODED AND WASHED OUT. 4.70 INCHES OF RAIN SINCE MIDNIGHT AND TOTAL RAINFALL OF 7 INCHES REPORTED BY SPOTTER.

EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED SEVERAL COUNTY ROADS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING AND HEAVY RAIN. THESE ROAD CLOSURES INCLUDED EFFENBECK...BRADY/MOOREFIELD 14 MILES SOUTH OF JEFFREY RESERVOIR...FOX CREEK ROAD AND CUT CREEK ROAD.

FLOODING REPORTED ON WEST SIDE OF CHAPPELL NEAR FAIRGROUNDS. LODGEPOLE CREEK REPORTED OUT OF ITS BANKS BY LAW ENFORCEMENT. LOWLAND FLOODING WAS OCCURRING IN THIS AREA. MINOR ROAD FLOODING IN CHAPPELL ON WEST END OF TOWN.
DEPUTY REPORTED SHALLOW WATER DEPTH RUNNING OVER SOUTH BUFFALO BILL ROAD.
WATER REPORTED OVER A GRAVEL COUNTY ROAD EAST OF ONEILL. OBSERVER REPORTS 3.93 INCHES OF RAIN IN ONEILL.

HAIL WAS STILL ONGOING AT TIME OF REPORT.
SHEETS OF HEAVY RAIN ALSO REPORTED BY TRAINED SPOTTER.
